What "WebGL" Actually Means

Table
Copy
TermMeaningOrigin
WebRuns in browserDistinguishes from desktop OpenGL
GLGraphics LibraryDirect descendant of OpenGL (1992)
OpenGL ES 2.0/3.0Embedded Systems profileMobile-optimized subset of OpenGL
WebGL 1.0 = JavaScript bindings for OpenGL ES 2.0 (2007 mobile standard) WebGL 2.0 = JavaScript bindings for OpenGL ES 3.0 (modern mobile)

The Fundamental Difference: Retained Mode vs Immediate Mode

plain
Copy
CANVAS 2D (Immediate Mode)              WEBGL (Retained Mode + Shaders)
─────────────────────────               ─────────────────────────────────
You say: "Draw a red circle here"       You say: "Here's mesh data, 
Browser: *immediately draws pixels*             here's how to color it"
                                        GPU: *stores data, executes 
                                              shader for every pixel*

No objects remembered                   Geometry buffered on GPU
Each frame = redraw everything          Each frame = re-execute shaders
CPU does the work                       GPU does the work (parallel)

WebGL Architecture (The Full Stack)

plain
Copy
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     WEBGL RENDERING PIPELINE                     │
│                                                                  │
│  JAVASCRIPT LAYER (CPU)                                          │
│  ├── Create context: canvas.getContext('webgl2')                │
│  ├── Compile shaders (GLSL text → GPU programs)                 │
│  ├── Create buffers (vertex data → GPU memory)                  │
│  └── Issue draw calls: gl.drawArrays()                          │
│                                                                  │
│  GRAPHICS DRIVER (Browser + OS)                                  │
│  ├── Translate WebGL calls → GPU-specific commands              │
│  ├── Memory management (buffer uploads)                         │
│  └── Shader compilation to GPU machine code                     │
│                                                                  │
│  GPU HARDWARE (Parallel Processor)                               │
│  ├── Vertex Shader: Transform 3D positions → 2D screen          │
│  ├── Rasterization: Convert triangles → pixels                  │
│  ├── Fragment Shader: Calculate color for each pixel            │
│  └── Framebuffer: Final pixel output                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

What Are Shaders?

Shaders = Programs that run on the GPU, written in GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language)
Table
Copy
Shader TypeRuns OnPurposeAnalogy
Vertex ShaderEach vertex (corner point)Position, transformation"Where is this point on screen?"
Fragment ShaderEach pixel (fragment)Color, texture, lighting"What color is this pixel?"

GLSL Shader Example (What We'd Write for v2)

glsl
Copy
// VERTEX SHADER: Position the letters
#version 300 es
in vec2 a_position;      // Letter corner (0,0 to 1,1)
in vec2 a_instancePos;   // Letter position in world
in float a_rotation;     // Letter rotation
in vec3 a_color;         // Letter color

uniform mat3 u_camera;   // View transformation

out vec3 v_color;        // Pass to fragment shader

void main() {
  // Rotate
  float c = cos(a_rotation);
  float s = sin(a_rotation);
  mat2 rotation = mat2(c, -s, s, c);
  
  // Position with rotation
  vec2 pos = rotation * a_position + a_instancePos;
  
  // Transform to screen space
  vec3 screenPos = u_camera * vec3(pos, 1.0);
  
  gl_Position = vec4(screenPos.xy, 0.0, 1.0);
  v_color = a_color;
}
glsl
Copy
// FRAGMENT SHADER: Color each pixel
#version 300 es
precision highp float;

in vec3 v_color;         // From vertex shader
out vec4 outColor;       // Final pixel color

void main() {
  // Add glow effect based on distance from center
  vec2 center = gl_PointCoord - vec2(0.5);
  float dist = length(center);
  float glow = 1.0 - smoothstep(0.3, 0.5, dist);
  
  outColor = vec4(v_color * glow, 1.0);
}

JavaScript WebGL Setup (Boilerplate)

JavaScript
Copy
// Getting WebGL context
const gl = canvas.getContext('webgl2');

// Creating shader program
function createShader(gl, type, source) {
  const shader = gl.createShader(type);
  gl.shaderSource(shader, source);
  gl.compileShader(shader);
  return shader;
}

const vs = createShader(gl, gl.VERTEX_SHADER, vertexShaderSource);
const fs = createShader(gl, gl.FRAGMENT_SHADER, fragmentShaderSource);

const program = gl.createProgram();
gl.attachShader(program, vs);
gl.attachShader(program, fs);
gl.linkProgram(program);

// Creating geometry buffer (GPU memory)
const positionBuffer = gl.createBuffer();
gl.bindBuffer(gl.ARRAY_BUFFER, positionBuffer);
gl.bufferData(gl.ARRAY_BUFFER, new Float32Array([
  0, 0,  // Triangle 1
  1, 0,
  0, 1,
  1, 1   // Triangle 2 (quad)
]), gl.STATIC_DRAW);

// Instanced rendering (draw 1000 letters in one call!)
gl.drawArraysInstanced(gl.TRIANGLES, 0, 6, 1000);

PART 3: DETAILED COMPARISON

Table
Copy
AspectCanvas 2DWebGLWhy the Difference
API StyleHigh-level, immediateLow-level, retained2D = ease of use; WebGL = control
Learning CurveDaysMonths2D abstracts math; WebGL exposes GPU
Performance (100 letters)60fps60fpsBoth fine at small scale
Performance (10,000 letters)5fps60fps2D CPU-bound; WebGL GPU-parallel
Effects (glow, blur)Built-in (shadowBlur)Manual shader code2D convenient; WebGL flexible
3D capabilityFake it with transformsNativeWebGL designed for 3D
Text renderingNative, high qualityTexture atlas or SDF2D uses system fonts; WebGL needs bitmaps
DebuggingEasy (visual)Hard (shaders opaque)2D = WYSIWYG; WebGL = black box
Bundle Size0kb (built-in)~50-200kb (Three.js)2D free; WebGL needs library usually

PART 4: WHY WE CHOSE CANVAS 2D FOR V1

The Decision Matrix

plain
Copy
Requirements for Kinetic Type Swarm v1:
✓ 100-500 letters maximum
✓ 2D only (no 3D depth needed)
✓ Text quality important (system fonts)
✓ Glow effects needed
✓ Rapid development (MVP timeline)
✓ Team expertise (React, not GLSL)

Canvas 2D Score: 9/10
WebGL Score: 4/10 (overkill, slower dev)

Specific Reasons

Table
Copy
ReasonExplanation
Particle count500 letters × 60fps = Canvas 2D handles easily. WebGL shines at 10,000+
Text renderingCanvas 2D: ctx.fillText() uses system fonts, crisp at all sizes. WebGL: must rasterize text to textures or use Signed Distance Fields (complex)
Glow effectsCanvas 2D: ctx.shadowBlur = 15 one line. WebGL: custom blur shader or post-processing pipeline
Development velocityCanvas 2D: productive in hours. WebGL: days to render first triangle correctly
DebuggingCanvas 2D: see immediate results. WebGL: black screen, check console, debug shaders

When We'll Switch to WebGL (v2)

plain
Copy
Trigger conditions for WebGL upgrade:
□ Need 5,000+ simultaneous letters
□ Add 3D depth/rotation (letters tumbling in space)
□ Post-processing effects (bloom, motion blur, distortion)
□ GPU particle systems (fluids, flocking behaviors)
□ VR/AR support (WebXR requires WebGL)

PART 5: THE NAMING CONVENTION DEEP DIVE

Why "Canvas" and Not "Bitmap" or "Surface"?

Table
Copy
TermWhy Not UsedWhy "Canvas" Won
BitmapToo technical, implies static image"Canvas" suggests active creation
SurfaceUsed in SDL/DirectX, too game-dev specific"Canvas" appeals to artists + programmers
DrawableAndroid term, too platform-specific"Canvas" is web-standard, neutral
LayerPhotoshop concept, implies stacking"Canvas" is single, unified space
Historical context: Apple introduced <canvas> in Safari 1.3 (2004), inspired by Mac OS X's Core Graphics. Mozilla adopted it for Firefox 1.5 (2005). Became HTML5 standard (2014).

Why "2D" and Not "2-D" or "TwoD"?

Table
Copy
FormatUsageWhy
2DAPI string: getContext('2d')Lowercase, no hyphen = easier to type, parse
2-DWritten EnglishHyphenated adjective (rare in code)
TwoDVariable names (twoDContext)CamelCase alternative in JavaScript
2dContext stringCase-sensitive match in spec
The string '2d' is lowercase because:
  • HTML attributes historically lowercase
  • Easier comparison: contextType.toLowerCase() === '2d'
  • Matches other context strings: 'webgl', 'bitmaprenderer' (all lowercase)

Why "WebGL" and Not "Web3D" or "Canvas3D"?

Table
Copy
AlternativeWhy RejectedWhy "WebGL" Chosen
Web3DToo generic, trademark issues"GL" signals OpenGL heritage
Canvas3DWould imply same API as 2D, just 3DCompletely different programming model
OpenWebGLRedundant (OpenGL already has Open)"Web" prefix sufficient for web platform
3D CanvasSame element, different API = confusingDistinct name = distinct mental model
"WebGL" acknowledges:
  • Web: Platform (browser)
  • GL: Lineage (OpenGL ES)
  • No version number: WebGL 1.0 and 2.0 both use same name in detection

PART 6: PRACTICAL IMPLEMENTATION IN OUR PROJECT

Our Canvas 2D Setup (Production-Ready)

JavaScript
Copy
// src/engine/Canvas2DEngine.js

export class Canvas2DEngine {
  constructor(canvas) {
    this.canvas = canvas;
    this.ctx = canvas.getContext('2d', {
      alpha: false,              // No transparency needed (black background)
      desynchronized: true,      // Reduce latency (Chrome only)
      preserveDrawingBuffer: false // Allow optimization (can't read pixels)
    });
    
    // High-DPI display support
    this.dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
    this.resize();
  }

  resize() {
    // Logical size (CSS pixels)
    const rect = this.canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    
    // Actual pixel buffer size (physical pixels)
    this.canvas.width = rect.width * this.dpr;
    this.canvas.height = rect.height * this.dpr;
    
    // Scale context so 1 unit = 1 CSS pixel
    this.ctx.scale(this.dpr, this.dpr);
    
    this.width = rect.width;
    this.height = rect.height;
  }

  render(letters, mouse) {
    const ctx = this.ctx;
    
    // Clear with fade for motion trails
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(10, 10, 10, 0.3)';
    ctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.width, this.height);
    
    // Batch by style to minimize state changes
    ctx.font = 'bold 32px monospace';
    ctx.textAlign = 'center';
    ctx.textBaseline = 'middle';
    
    letters.forEach(letter => {
      // Skip off-screen letters (culling)
      if (letter.x < -50 || letter.x > this.width + 50 ||
          letter.y < -50 || letter.y > this.height + 50) {
        return;
      }
      
      ctx.save();
      
      // Dynamic color based on velocity
      const speed = Math.hypot(letter.vx, letter.vy);
      const hue = 180 + Math.min(speed * 10, 60); // Cyan to purple
      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${hue}, 100%, 50%)`;
      
      // Glow intensity based on speed
      ctx.shadowBlur = 10 + speed * 2;
      ctx.shadowColor = ctx.fillStyle;
      
      // Transform
      ctx.translate(letter.x, letter.y);
      ctx.rotate(letter.vx * 0.05);
      
      // Draw
      ctx.fillText(letter.char, 0, 0);
      
      ctx.restore();
    });
    
    // Draw mouse cursor
    if (mouse.x !== null) {
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(mouse.x, mouse.y, 20, 0, Math.PI * 2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(0, 255, 255, 0.5)';
      ctx.lineWidth = 2;
      ctx.stroke();
    }
  }
}

WebGL Upgrade Path (Future Architecture)

JavaScript
Copy
// src/engine/WebGLEngine.js (future)

export class WebGLEngine {
  constructor(canvas) {
    this.gl = canvas.getContext('webgl2', {
      antialias: true,
      alpha: false,
      powerPreference: 'high-performance'
    });
    
    this.initShaders();
    this.initGeometry();
    this.initBuffers();
  }

  initShaders() {
    // Compile vertex/fragment shaders
    // Create program
    // Get attribute/uniform locations
  }

  initGeometry() {
    // Single quad for all letters (instanced rendering)
    // Position buffer: 4 corners × 2 floats
    // Instance buffer: position, rotation, color, character index
  }

  render(letters, mouse) {
    // Update instance buffer with letter data
    // Single draw call: gl.drawArraysInstanced(gl.TRIANGLES, 0, 6, letters.length)
    // Post-processing for bloom effect
  }
}

PART 7: SUMMARY TABLE

Table
Copy
ConceptCanvas 2DWebGL
Mental Model"I draw shapes""I configure GPU pipeline"
Code Complexity10 lines = animation100 lines = first triangle
Performance Ceiling~3,000 objects~100,000+ objects
Best ForUI, 2D games, charts3D, particles, shaders, VR
Our v1 Choice✅ YES❌ No (overkill)
Our v2 TriggerPerformance limitsScale requirements

🎯 NEXT DECISION

Which rendering deep-dive interests you?
Table
Copy
OptionTopicWhat You'll Learn
ACanvas 2D optimizationDirty rectangles, offscreen caching, composite layers
BWebGL from scratchWrite shaders, buffers, instancing — no libraries
CThree.js integrationHigh-level WebGL for our letter system
DHybrid approachCanvas 2D for text, WebGL for particles
EWebGPU (future)Next-gen API, replaces WebGL
Each includes working code you can run immediately.
now how do I implement all of this. here are my tool. wix in browser. visual studio. react , css. html and so on. explain step by step. what is the best method and suggest other tools, mprocedure if any. and how to get all of this to go live
Edit
Copy
Share
I'll guide you through building and deploying this project step-by-step with your existing tools, plus exactly what to add and why.

🛠️ YOUR TOOLKIT ASSESSMENT

Table
Copy
Tool You HaveRole in ProjectLimitationSolution
WixWebsite builder, hostingClosed ecosystem, no backend controlUse for marketing page only, NOT the app
Visual StudioCode editorHeavy for web devSwitch to VS Code (free, lighter, better JS support)
ReactFrontend framework✅ Perfect, keep thisNone needed
CSSStyling✅ Perfect, keep thisConsider Tailwind for speed
HTMLStructure✅ Perfect, keep thisJSX in React replaces manual HTML
What You Need to Add:
  • VS Code (replace Visual Studio for web work)
  • Node.js (JavaScript runtime)
  • Vite (build tool, replaces Create React App)
  • Git + GitHub (version control)
  • Vercel or Netlify (free hosting, superior to Wix for apps)

📋 PHASE 1: ENVIRONMENT SETUP (30 minutes)

Step 1: Install Required Software

Download and install in this order:
plain
Copy
1. Node.js (LTS version)
   → https://nodejs.org (download "LTS", not "Current")
   → Includes npm (Node Package Manager)
   
2. VS Code
   → https://code.visualstudio.com
   → Lighter than Visual Studio, built for web dev
   
3. Git
   → https://git-scm.com/downloads
   → For version control and deployment
Verify installations (open terminal in VS Code: `Ctrl + ``):
bash
Copy
node --version      # Should show v18.x.x or higher
npm --version       # Should show 9.x.x or higher
git --version       # Should show 2.x.x or higher

Step 2: Configure VS Code

Install these extensions (click Extensions icon in left sidebar):
Table
Copy
ExtensionPurposeWhy
ES7+ React/Redux/React-Native snippetsCode shortcutsType rafce → creates React component
Prettier - Code: formatterAuto-format codeConsistent style, no manual formatting
Auto Rename TagSync HTML/JSX tagsChange <div> → <span>, closing tag auto-updates
Bracket Pair ColorizerVisual bracket matchingPrevents nesting errors
Live ServerLocal previewSee changes instantly (though Vite has this built-in)
Configure settings (Ctrl + , then click icon to open settings.json):
JSON
Copy
{
  "editor.formatOnSave": true,
  "editor.defaultFormatter": "esbenp.prettier-vscode",
  "emmet.includeLanguages": {
    "javascript": "javascriptreact"
  },
  "files.autoSave": "afterDelay"
}

📋 PHASE 2: PROJECT CREATION (20 minutes)

Step 3: Create React Project with Vite

Why Vite over Create React App (CRA)?
  • 10x faster startup
  • Instant hot module replacement
  • Optimized production builds
  • Modern standard (CRA is deprecated)
In VS Code terminal:
bash
Copy
# Navigate to where you want the project
cd Documents

# Create project (choose React + JavaScript)
npm create vite@latest kinetic-swarm -- --template react

# Enter project
cd kinetic-swarm

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Install additional packages we need
npm install zustand socket.io-client

# Start development server
npm run dev
What just happened:
plain
Copy
kinetic-swarm/
├── node_modules/     # Dependencies (auto-generated, don't touch)
├── public/           # Static assets (favicon, etc.)
├── src/
│   ├── assets/       # Images, fonts
│   ├── App.jsx       # Main component
│   ├── main.jsx      # Entry point
│   └── index.css     # Global styles
├── index.html        # HTML template
├── package.json      # Project config
└── vite.config.js    # Build tool config
Open browser: Go to http://localhost:5173 (Vite shows the URL)
You should see the default Vite + React page.

📋 PHASE 3: BUILD THE APPLICATION (2-3 hours)

Step 4: Create Folder Structure

In VS Code, create these folders/files:
plain
Copy
src/
├── components/
│   ├── Canvas/
│   │   ├── Canvas.jsx
│   │   └── Canvas.css
│   ├── UI/
│   │   ├── UI.jsx
│   │   └── UI.css
│   └── Letter/
│       └── Letter.js
├── hooks/
│   ├── useMouse.js
│   ├── useLetters.js
│   └── useAnimation.js
├── stores/
│   └── useGameStore.js
├── utils/
│   └── vec.js
├── engine/
│   └── Canvas2DEngine.js
├── App.jsx
├── App.css
└── main.jsx
VS Code shortcut: Right-click src → New Folder, or use terminal:
bash
Copy
mkdir -p src/components/Canvas src/components/UI src/components/Letter src/hooks src/stores src/utils src/engine

Step 5: Write the Code Files

Copy each file exactly (I'll provide complete, working code):

src/utils/vec.js (Vector math library)

JavaScript
Copy
export const vec = {
  add: (a, b) => ({ x: a.x + b.x, y: a.y + b.y }),
  sub: (a, b) => ({ x: a.x - b.x, y: a.y - b.y }),
  mult: (v, s) => ({ x: v.x * s, y: v.y * s }),
  mag: (v) => Math.hypot(v.x, v.y),
  norm: (v) => {
    const m = Math.hypot(v.x, v.y) || 1;
    return { x: v.x / m, y: v.y / m };
  },
  limit: (v, max) => {
    const m = Math.hypot(v.x, v.y);
    if (m > max) return { x: (v.x / m) * max, y: (v.y / m) * max };
    return v;
  },
  dist: (a, b) => Math.hypot(a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y),
};

src/hooks/useMouse.js (Track mouse position)

JavaScript
Copy
import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

export function useMouse() {
  const mouse = useRef({ x: null, y: null, isActive: false });

  useEffect(() => {
    const handleMove = (e) => {
      mouse.current = {
        x: e.clientX,
        y: e.clientY,
        isActive: true,
      };
    };

    const handleLeave = () => {
      mouse.current = { x: null, y: null, isActive: false };
    };

    window.addEventListener('mousemove', handleMove);
    window.addEventListener('mouseleave', handleLeave);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener('mousemove', handleMove);
      window.removeEventListener('mouseleave', handleLeave);
    };
  }, []);

  return mouse;
}

src/hooks/useLetters.js (Letter physics)

JavaScript
Copy
import { useRef, useCallback } from 'react';
import { vec } from '../utils/vec';

const CONFIG = {
  maxSpeed: 8,
  attractRadius: 150,
  attractStrength: 0.15,
  returnStrength: 0.03,
  friction: 0.94,
  scatterRadius: 200,
};

export function useLetters(word, canvasSize) {
  const letters = useRef([]);

  const measureText = (text) => {
    const ctx = document.createElement('canvas').getContext('2d');
    ctx.font = '32px monospace';
    return ctx.measureText(text).width;
  };

  const init = useCallback(() => {
    if (!canvasSize.width || !canvasSize.height) return;

    const centerX = canvasSize.width / 2;
    const centerY = canvasSize.height / 2;
    const totalWidth = measureText(word);
    let currentX = centerX - totalWidth / 2;

    letters.current = [...word].map((char) => {
      const charWidth = measureText(char);
      const home = { x: currentX + charWidth / 2, y: centerY };
      currentX += charWidth;

      const scatter = {
        x: (Math.random() - 0.5) * CONFIG.scatterRadius * 2,
        y: (Math.random() - 0.5) * CONFIG.scatterRadius * 2,
      };

      return {
        char,
        home,
        pos: { x: home.x + scatter.x, y: home.y + scatter.y },
        vel: { x: 0, y: 0 },
        acc: { x: 0, y: 0 },
      };
    });
  }, [word, canvasSize]);

  const update = useCallback((mouse) => {
    letters.current.forEach((letter) => {
      letter.acc = { x: 0, y: 0 };

      if (mouse.isActive) {
        const toMouse = vec.sub(mouse, letter.pos);
        const d = vec.mag(toMouse);

        if (d < CONFIG.attractRadius && d > 0) {
          const force = (1 - d / CONFIG.attractRadius) * CONFIG.attractStrength;
          letter.acc = vec.add(letter.acc, vec.mult(vec.norm(toMouse), force));
        }
      } else {
        const toHome = vec.sub(letter.home, letter.pos);
        letter.acc = vec.add(letter.acc, vec.mult(toHome, CONFIG.returnStrength));
      }

      letter.vel = vec.add(letter.vel, letter.acc);
      letter.vel = vec.mult(letter.vel, CONFIG.friction);
      letter.vel = vec.limit(letter.vel, CONFIG.maxSpeed);
      letter.pos = vec.add(letter.pos, letter.vel);
    });
  }, []);

  return { letters, init, update };
}

src/engine/Canvas2DEngine.js (Rendering engine)

JavaScript
Copy
export class Canvas2DEngine {
  constructor(canvas) {
    this.canvas = canvas;
    this.ctx = canvas.getContext('2d', {
      alpha: false,
      desynchronized: true,
    });
    this.dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
    this.resize();
  }

  resize() {
    const rect = this.canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    this.canvas.width = rect.width * this.dpr;
    this.canvas.height = rect.height * this.dpr;
    this.ctx.scale(this.dpr, this.dpr);
    this.width = rect.width;
    this.height = rect.height;
  }

  render(letters, mouse) {
    const ctx = this.ctx;

    // Trail effect
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(10, 10, 10, 0.3)';
    ctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.width, this.height);

    ctx.font = 'bold 32px monospace';
    ctx.textAlign = 'center';
    ctx.textBaseline = 'middle';

    letters.current.forEach((letter) => {
      // Culling
      if (
        letter.x < -50 ||
        letter.x > this.width + 50 ||
        letter.y < -50 ||
        letter.y > this.height + 50
      )
        return;

      ctx.save();

      const speed = Math.hypot(letter.vel.x, letter.vel.y);
      const hue = 180 + Math.min(speed * 10, 60);
      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${hue}, 100%, 50%)`;
      ctx.shadowBlur = 10 + speed * 2;
      ctx.shadowColor = ctx.fillStyle;

      ctx.translate(letter.pos.x, letter.pos.y);
      ctx.rotate(letter.vel.x * 0.05);
      ctx.fillText(letter.char, 0, 0);

      ctx.restore();
    });

    // Mouse cursor
    if (mouse.x !== null) {
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(mouse.x, mouse.y, 20, 0, Math.PI * 2);
      ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(0, 255, 255, 0.5)';
      ctx.lineWidth = 2;
      ctx.stroke();
    }
  }
}

src/components/Canvas/Canvas.jsx (Main canvas component)

JavaScript
Copy
import { useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react';
import { useMouse } from '../../hooks/useMouse';
import { useLetters } from '../../hooks/useLetters';
import { Canvas2DEngine } from '../../engine/Canvas2DEngine';
import './Canvas.css';

export function Canvas({ word }) {
  const canvasRef = useRef(null);
  const engineRef = useRef(null);
  const mouse = useMouse();
  const rafRef = useRef();

  const [size, setSize] = useState({ width: 0, height: 0 });
  const { letters, init, update } = useLetters(word, size);

  // Initialize engine
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!canvasRef.current) return;
    engineRef.current = new Canvas2DEngine(canvasRef.current);
    
    const handleResize = () => {
      engineRef.current.resize();
      setSize({
        width: engineRef.current.width,
        height: engineRef.current.height,
      });
    };

    handleResize();
    window.addEventListener('resize', handleResize);
    return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', handleResize);
  }, []);

  // Initialize letters when word or size changes
  useEffect(() => {
    init();
  }, [word, size, init]);

  // Animation loop
  const animate = useCallback(() => {
    if (engineRef.current) {
      update(mouse.current);
      engineRef.current.render(letters, mouse.current);
    }
    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(animate);
  }, [update, letters, mouse]);

  useEffect(() => {
    rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(animate);
    return () => cancelAnimationFrame(rafRef.current);
  }, [animate]);

  return <canvas ref={canvasRef} className="canvas" />;
}

// Add useState import
import { useState } from 'react';

src/components/Canvas/Canvas.css

css
Copy
.canvas {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  display: block;
  cursor: none;
  background: #0a0a0a;
}

src/components/UI/UI.jsx (Control panel)

JavaScript
Copy
import './UI.css';

const WORDS = ['REACT', 'SWARM', 'PHYSICS', 'CODE', 'CREATE', 'FLOW'];

export function UI({ currentWord, onChangeWord }) {
  return (
    <div className="ui-container">
      <div className="ui-panel">
        <h3>Current Word</h3>
        <div className="word-display">{currentWord}</div>

        <h4>Word Bank</h4>
        <div className="word-grid">
          {WORDS.map((w) => (
            <button
              key={w}
              className={`word-btn ${w === currentWord ? 'active' : ''}`}
              onClick={() => onChangeWord(w)}
            >
              {w}
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>

        <div className="hint">
          Move mouse to attract letters
          <br />
          Leave canvas to form word
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

src/components/UI/UI.css

css
Copy
.ui-container {
  position: absolute;
  top: 20px;
  right: 20px;
  z-index: 100;
  font-family: 'SF Mono', Monaco, monospace;
}

.ui-panel {
  background: rgba(10, 10, 10, 0.95);
  border: 1px solid #0ff;
  border-radius: 12px;
  padding: 20px;
  min-width: 200px;
  backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
}

.ui-panel h3,
.ui-panel h4 {
  color: #0ff;
  margin: 0 0 12px 0;
  font-size: 14px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 1px;
}

.word-display {
  font-size: 24px;
  color: #fff;
  text-align: center;
  padding: 12px;
  background: rgba(0, 255, 255, 0.1);
  border-radius: 8px;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
  letter-spacing: 4px;
}

.word-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
  gap: 8px;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.word-btn {
  padding: 10px;
  border: 1px solid #333;
  background: transparent;
  color: #888;
  border-radius: 6px;
  cursor: pointer;
  font-family: inherit;
  font-size: 12px;
  transition: all 0.2s;
}

.word-btn:hover {
  border-color: #0ff;
  color: #0ff;
}

.word-btn.active {
  background: #0ff;
  color: #000;
  border-color: #0ff;
}

.hint {
  color: #666;
  font-size: 11px;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1.5;
  padding-top: 16px;
  border-top: 1px solid #222;
}

src/App.jsx (Root component)

JavaScript
Copy
import { useState } from 'react';
import { Canvas } from './components/Canvas/Canvas';
import { UI } from './components/UI/UI';
import './App.css';

function App() {
  const [word, setWord] = useState('REACT');

  return (
    <div className="app">
      <Canvas word={word} />
      <UI currentWord={word} onChangeWord={setWord} />
    </div>
  );
}

export default App;

src/App.css

css
Copy
* {
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

html,
body {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  overflow: hidden;
  background: #0a0a0a;
}

.app {
  width: 100vw;
  height: 100vh;
  position: relative;
}
